ConstructorArguments: Draws a single model at the given Transform immediately.

Documentation.Summary: Draws a single model at the given Transform immediately.

Params.transform: Transform to draw the model at
Params.attributes: Optional attributes to apply only for this draw call

ConstructorArguments: Draws multiple instances of a model using GPU instancing, assuming standard implemented shaders. Use `GetTransformMatrix( int instance )` in shaders to access the instance transform. There is a limit of 1,048,576 transform slots per frame when using this method.

Draws multiple instances of a model using GPU instancing with the number of instances being provided by indirect draw arguments. Use `SV_InstanceID` semantic in shaders to access the rendered instance.

Documentation.Summary:
Draws multiple instances of a model using GPU instancing with the number of instances being provided by indirect draw arguments.
Use `SV_InstanceID` semantic in shaders to access the rendered instance.

Params.buffer: The GPU buffer containing the DrawIndirectArguments
Params.bufferOffset: Optional offset in the GPU buffer
Params.attributes: Optional attributes to apply only for this draw call

ConstructorArguments: Draws multiple instances of a model using GPU instancing. This is similar to CommandList.DrawModelInstancedIndirect(Model,GpuBuffer,int,RenderAttributes), except the count is provided from the CPU rather than via a GPU buffer. Use `SV_InstanceID` semantic in shaders to access the rendered instance.

Documentation.Summary:
Draws multiple instances of a model using GPU instancing.
This is similar to <see cref="M:Sandbox.Rendering.CommandList.DrawModelInstancedIndirect(Sandbox.Model,Sandbox.GpuBuffer,System.Int32,Sandbox.RenderAttributes)" />,
except the count is provided from the CPU rather than via a GPU buffer.
            
Use `SV_InstanceID` semantic in shaders to access the rendered instance.

Params.count: The number of instances to draw
Params.attributes: Optional attributes to apply only for this draw call

ConstructorArguments: Draws geometry using a vertex buffer and material.
Draws indexed geometry using vertex and index buffers.
Draws instanced geometry using a vertex buffer and indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.
Draws instanced geometry using a vertex buffer and indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Draws instanced geometry using a vertex buffer and indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.
Documentation.Remarks: Vertex data is accessed in shader through buffer attribute and SV_VertexID.

Params.material: The material to use for rendering.
Params.indirectBuffer: The GPU buffer containing indirect draw arguments.
Params.bufferOffset: Optional byte offset into the indirect buffer.
Params.attributes: Optional render attributes to apply only for this draw call.
Params.primitiveType: The type of primitives to render. Defaults to triangles.

ConstructorArguments: Draws instanced indexed geometry using indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.
Draws instanced indexed geometry using indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Draws instanced indexed geometry using indirect draw arguments stored in a GPU buffer.
Documentation.Remarks: Vertex data is accessed in shader through buffer attribute and SV_VertexID.

Params.indexBuffer: The GPU buffer containing index data.
Params.material: The material to use for rendering.
Params.indirectBuffer: The GPU buffer containing indirect draw arguments.
Params.bufferOffset: Optional byte offset into the indirect buffer.
Params.attributes: Optional render attributes to apply only for this draw call.
Params.primitiveType: The type of primitives to render. Defaults to triangles.

ConstructorArguments: Draws indexed geometry with instancing. Each instance shares the same index buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Draws indexed geometry with instancing. Each instance shares the same index buffer.

ConstructorArguments: Get a screen sized temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.

Documentation.Summary: Get a screen sized temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.
Documentation.Return: A RenderTarget that is ready to render to.

Params.name: The name of the render target handle.
Params.sizeFactor: Divide the screen size by this factor. 2 would be half screen sized. 1 for full screen sized.
Params.format: The format for the color buffer. If set to default we'll use whatever the current pipeline is using.
Params.numMips: Number of mips you want in this texture. You probably don't want this unless you want to generate mips in a second pass.

ConstructorArguments: Get a screen sized temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.

Documentation.Summary: Get a screen sized temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.
Documentation.Return: A RenderTarget that is ready to render to.

Params.name: The name of the render target handle.
Params.sizeFactor: Divide the screen size by this factor. 2 would be half screen sized. 1 for full screen sized.
Params.colorFormat: The format for the color buffer. If set to default we'll use whatever the current pipeline is using.
Params.depthFormat: The format for the depth buffer.
Params.msaa: The number of msaa samples you'd like. Msaa render textures are a pain in the ass so you're probably gonna regret trying to use this.
Params.numMips: Number of mips you want in this texture. You probably don't want this unless you want to generate mips in a second pass.

ConstructorArguments: Get a temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.

Documentation.Summary: Get a temporary render target. You should release the returned handle when you're done to return the textures to the pool.
Documentation.Return: A RenderTarget that is ready to render to.

Params.name: The name of the render target handle.
Params.width: Width of the render target you want.
Params.height: Height of the render target you want.
Params.colorFormat: The format for the color buffer. If set to default we'll use whatever the current pipeline is using.
Params.depthFormat: The format for the depth buffer.
Params.msaa: The number of msaa samples you'd like. Msaa render textures are a pain in the ass so you're probably gonna regret trying to use this.
Params.numMips: Number of mips you want in this texture. You probably don't want this unless you want to generate mips in a second pass.

ConstructorArguments: Executes a barrier transition for the given GPU Buffer Resource. Transitions the buffer resource from a known source state to a specified destination state.
